Priority touch-up list

  • **SPINE_GAP** (65 co's) — Tier-2 strike list; see `funding_round_gaps_targets_*.jsonl`. Highest signal-to-effort.
  • **ALL_UNKNOWN_DATES** (80 co's) — Dataminr-class. Wikipedia / press archive likely to yield month-precision.
  • **BOTH_GAPS** (71 co's) — needs full re-source on at least one round.
  • **DATE_GAPS** (15 co's) — anchor exists (amount); date-precision agent run.
  • **264 non-USD rounds** awaiting FX table (task #62) to canonicalize amount_usd_millions.
  • **NO_ROUNDS triage** (267 co's) — most are legit products/divisions/bootstrapped (see `_classify_no_rounds.py`).

HN Hiring history ⚠️ partial ingest

20,767 of 21,791 HN aggregated companies (95.3%) are NOT YET ingested into the corpus. When cohort-9+ detail-pass closes the gap, this banner flips to ✓ but the analytics below stay. Already in corpus (alias-aware): 1,024.

Computed from data/hn_hiring_aggregated.jsonl (21,791 unique companies). Each company has BOTH runs[] (continuous posting streaks) AND gaps[] (silent periods). Resurfacing rate: 5,273 (24.2%) of companies had at least one ≥6-month silent period.

Optional fields

🗑 Wipe Local Data

This deletes all your tracker data from this browser.

Useful for: leaving a shared device, finishing a hotel-laptop session, or just wanting nothing left behind. Not because we're trying to keep you here.

Before you wipe:

  • Did you export first? Use the Export JSON button.
  • Are you sure this is the only device with this data?
  • Backup keys (.prev, .recovery.*) will also be wiped.

After wiping, this page will reload to clear in-memory state. There is no undo.

Import — choose mode

Loading…

How should conflicts (same company already in your browser) be handled?

Interactions Diary · Activity Timeline

Loading…
🔒 Your data, your browser. That's it.
  • Lives in: this browser's localStorage only
  • Sent to: nowhere. No network calls. No analytics. No telemetry vendor.
  • Visible to: you (and anyone with access to this browser profile)
  • Backed up by: only you, via the Export JSON button above
  • Deleted by: clearing browser storage OR the "Clear all for this co" button in each row's modal

Why this matters: this is a diary for sifting through eye-watering numbers of applications. Your starred companies, salary notes, rejection reasons, and contact details stay with you. The off-the-shelf trackers' business model is data aggregation — we're explicitly the opposite. Export frequently.

Threat model — what the browser does and doesn't protect against

The browser handles: cross-origin isolation. Other websites cannot read this page's localStorage. That's enforced by the same-origin policy.

The browser does NOT handle: same-origin XSS (if attacker JS runs on THIS page, it reads localStorage directly). Browser-extension reads with broad permissions. Physical access to your browser profile. Export-file leak.

Our mitigations: this page is static HTML with no external network calls and no third-party scripts — minimal XSS surface. Content Security Policy denies external connections (connect-src 'none'). The Wipe Data button below scrubs all keys including .prev and .recovery.* backups.

What we deliberately don't do: encrypt localStorage with a passphrase. Same-origin XSS would defeat it (attacker can call decrypt), but the prompt-every-load + lost-passphrase risks are real. If you need that level, encrypt the EXPORT file yourself with gpg or similar before storing it anywhere.

About browser extensions: a page cannot meaningfully block extensions you have installed. Content scripts run in an isolated world that bypasses page CSP, and any DOM-locking we tried would also break password managers (1Password, Bitwarden), dark-mode extensions (Dark Reader), writing aids (Grammarly), screen readers / read-aloud add-ons, OpenDyslexic injectors, high-contrast / color-blindness aids, and translation extensions. Locking out extensions to "protect" your data would lock out the same APIs assistive tech rides on. We refuse to take that trade.

For high-sensitivity use: the right control is at the browser profile level, not in this page:

  • Separate browser profile for sensitive work, with zero extensions installed
  • Incognito / Private window — most extensions disabled by default
  • chrome://extensions → Site access → restrict per-origin (modern Chromium lets you opt extensions out of specific origins)
  • Firefox Containers — isolate localStorage and extension access per container

For traveling / shared devices: wipe before you go. Re-import the export when you arrive. That's the right primitive.

Schema v1 (locked). Internal field name "interactions" (not "telemetry") — opens cleanly in DevTools so you can verify what's stored.
